label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to analyze this ethical dilemma where someone has the flu and an important project deadline is coming up. They have to decide between staying home and potentially delaying the project or going to work and risking spreading the illness. Let me break this down. First, the person has the flu, which is contagious. If they go to work, they might infect others. The company is small, so everyone's contribution is significant. That means if more people get sick, the project could be even more delayed. The project has a tight deadline, so time is of the essence. Ethical principles involved here might include things like autonomy (the right to make one's own decisions), beneficence (doing good), non-maleficence (not doing harm), and justice (fairness).
